metadata.note: Output used as-is without manual revision or selection.

causal_texts: The glass slipped from her hand. It fell to the floor. It broke into many pieces. She swept them up carefully.
He planted a seed in the soil. He watered it every morning. A small green sprout appeared. It grew into a tall plant.
The sky grew dark with heavy clouds. Rain began to pour down suddenly. The streets filled with rushing water. People opened their umbrellas quickly.
She lit a match near the candle. The wick caught the small flame. The candle glowed warmly in the dark. She placed it on the table.
The dog spotted a squirrel in the yard. It began to chase the animal. The squirrel scrambled up a tall tree. The dog barked at the trunk.
He turned the key in the ignition. The engine roared to life loudly. He shifted the car into gear. He drove away down the road.
The kettle began to whistle on the stove. She poured the hot water into a mug. The tea leaves steeped for a few minutes. She sipped the warm drink slowly.
A strong wind blew through the open window. The papers scattered across the desk. He rushed to gather them together. He placed a heavy book on top.
The alarm clock rang early in the morning. She stretched and climbed out of bed. She walked to the kitchen for coffee. She started her busy day.
The child dropped an ice cream cone. It landed on the hot pavement. The ice cream melted in the sun. The child began to cry.
He struck the nail with a hammer. The nail sank into the wooden board. He hung a picture on the wall. He stepped back to admire it.
The chef chopped the onions finely. He tossed them into a hot pan. The onions sizzled and turned golden. He added them to the soup.
She dialed the number on her phone. It rang several times before answering. Her friend picked up the call. They chatted happily for an hour.
The bread went into the toaster. The coils glowed a bright red. The toast popped up when finished. She spread butter across the surface.
Lightning flashed across the night sky. Thunder rumbled a moment later. The dog hid beneath the bed. It trembled until the storm passed.
He filled the bucket with soapy water. He dipped a sponge inside it. He scrubbed the dirty car carefully. The paint gleamed in the sunlight.
The gardener pulled the weeds from the bed. She loosened the soil with a fork. She planted rows of bright flowers. She watered them until dusk.
The baby reached for the colorful toy. It grabbed the rattle tightly. It shook the toy with delight. It giggled at the cheerful sound.
She folded the paper into a small square. She creased each edge carefully. She unfolded it into a paper crane. She set it on the shelf.
The runner laced up her shoes. She stretched her legs on the porch. She jogged along the quiet trail. She returned home feeling refreshed.
He opened the old wooden chest. Dust rose into the still air. He found a stack of letters inside. He read them by candlelight.
The cat crept toward the sleeping bird. It froze when a branch snapped. The bird flew away in alarm. The cat sat down, disappointed.
She mixed the flour and sugar together. She cracked two eggs into the bowl. She stirred the batter until smooth. She poured it into the pan.
The boy kicked the ball hard. It sailed over the fence. It landed in the neighbor's yard. He climbed over to retrieve it.
The waves crashed against the sandcastle. The walls began to crumble slowly. The tide swallowed the tower whole. Only smooth sand remained behind.
She switched on the old radio. Static crackled through the speaker. She turned the dial slowly. A clear song finally played.
The farmer scattered seeds across the field. Rain fell steadily for a week. Green shoots pushed through the earth. The field turned into golden wheat.
He blew air into the balloon. It swelled larger and larger. He tied the end in a knot. He released it into the sky.
The candle burned lower and lower. The wax pooled at the base. The flame flickered one last time. Darkness filled the quiet room.
She placed the letter in an envelope. She sealed it and added a stamp. She dropped it in the mailbox. The postman collected it later.
The spider spun a delicate web. A fly wandered into the strands. It struggled to break free. The spider approached its trapped meal.
He poured cement into the mold. It hardened over several hours. He removed the mold carefully. A solid brick remained behind.
The teacher wrote a question on the board. The students raised their hands eagerly. One child gave the correct answer. The teacher smiled with approval.
She dropped a stone into the pond. Ripples spread across the water. They widened into large circles. The surface grew calm again.
The mechanic opened the car's hood. He checked the oil and belts. He tightened a loose bolt. He closed the hood with satisfaction.
Snow fell softly through the night. It covered the rooftops in white. Children woke and cheered loudly. They rushed outside to play.
He struck a chord on the guitar. The notes echoed through the room. He hummed along to the melody. The song filled the evening air.
The baker kneaded the sticky dough. He let it rise for an hour. He shaped it into round loaves. He slid them into the oven.
She painted the fence a bright blue. The paint dried under the sun. She added a second coat carefully. The fence looked brand new.
The fisherman cast his line far. He waited quietly by the water. A fish tugged at the hook. He reeled it in slowly.
The volcano rumbled deep below. Smoke rose from its peak. Lava spilled down the slopes. The village fled to safety.
He wound the clock's spring tightly. The gears began to turn. The hands moved around the face. The clock chimed at noon.
She scattered crumbs on the ground. Birds gathered from every direction. They pecked at the tiny pieces. They flew off when satisfied.
The wave lifted the small boat. It rocked back and forth. The sailor gripped the rail tightly. The sea grew calm again.
He flipped the light switch on. The bulb flickered and buzzed. It glowed steadily after a moment. The room filled with warm light.
The seed cracked open underground. A tiny root reached downward. A green stem pushed upward. A leaf unfurled toward the light.
She poured milk into the glass. She added a scoop of powder. She stirred it until dissolved. She drank the sweet chocolate milk.
The dog dug a hole in the yard. It buried a bone inside. It covered the spot with dirt. It trotted away, content.
Rain soaked the dry riverbed. Water trickled between the rocks. The trickle became a rushing stream. The river flowed once more.
He lit the campfire with care. The flames grew tall and bright. Sparks drifted into the night. The friends gathered around it.
The bee landed on a flower. It gathered the sweet nectar. It flew back to the hive. It shared the harvest inside.
She sharpened her pencil to a point. She opened her sketchbook slowly. She drew a curving line. A landscape took shape.
The train pulled into the station. Passengers stepped onto the platform. The doors slid shut again. The train departed on time.
He poured batter onto the griddle. Bubbles formed on the surface. He flipped the pancake over. It turned a golden brown.
The wind carried a dandelion seed. It drifted over the meadow. It settled in fertile soil. A new flower bloomed there.
She wound thread around the spool. She threaded the needle carefully. She stitched the torn fabric. The tear vanished completely.
The sun sank below the horizon. The sky glowed orange and pink. Stars appeared one by one. Night settled over the land.
He stacked the wooden blocks high. The tower wobbled slightly. His hand bumped the top block. The whole tower tumbled down.
The chef seasoned the steak generously. He seared it in a hot pan. He let it rest a few minutes. He served it on a plate.
She opened the window each morning. Fresh air filled the room. Birdsong drifted inside. She breathed in deeply.
The dam held back the river. Cracks formed in the concrete. Water burst through the gaps. The valley flooded below.
He plugged in the old lamp. He flipped the switch expectantly. Nothing happened at first. The bulb finally glowed dimly.
The caterpillar spun a silk cocoon. It rested inside for weeks. Its body slowly transformed. A butterfly emerged at last.
She boiled the pasta in water. She drained it in a colander. She tossed it with warm sauce. She sprinkled cheese on top.
The hikers reached the mountain base. They climbed the winding path. They rested at the summit. They admired the wide view.
He wound the music box key. A gentle melody began. The tiny dancer spun around. The tune slowed and stopped.
The frost coated the window pane. Delicate patterns spread across it. The morning sun warmed the glass. The frost melted into droplets.
She planted bulbs in the autumn. Winter buried them in snow. Spring warmed the frozen ground. Tulips bloomed in bright colors.
The candle wax dripped down the side. It pooled onto the holder. It cooled into a hard lump. She peeled it off later.
He tossed a pebble into the air. It arced across the sky. It splashed into the lake. Ripples spread from the spot.
The chef cracked an egg into the pan. The white spread and sizzled. The yolk firmed in the heat. He slid it onto toast.
She wound the garden hose neatly. She hung it on the hook. She wiped her muddy hands. She stepped back inside.
The storm knocked down a tree. It blocked the narrow road. Workers arrived with chainsaws. They cleared the path quickly.
He sowed carrot seeds in rows. He covered them with soil. He watered the bed daily. Green tops sprouted after weeks.
The child built a fort of pillows. She crawled inside the tunnel. She read a book by flashlight. She fell asleep there.
The blacksmith heated the iron bar. It glowed a fiery orange. He hammered it into shape. He cooled it in water.
She lit the stove burner. She placed a pot on top. The water bubbled and boiled. Steam rose toward the ceiling.
The wind spun the pinwheel fast. Its colors blurred together. The breeze died down slowly. The pinwheel came to rest.
He shuffled the deck of cards. He dealt five to each player. They picked up their hands. The game began at once.
The tide pulled back from the shore. It exposed the wet sand. Crabs scurried across the flats. Seagulls swooped down to feed.
She poured wax into the mold. She set a wick in the center. The wax cooled and hardened. She had a new candle.
The rooster crowed at dawn. The farmer rose from bed. He fed the hungry animals. He began the morning chores.
He dipped the brush in paint. He swept it across the canvas. Colors blended into a scene. He signed his name below.
The apple ripened on the branch. It grew heavy and red. It fell to the grass. A child picked it up.
She wound the yarn into a ball. She cast stitches onto a needle. She knitted row after row. A warm scarf emerged.
The kite caught the strong wind. It rose high above the field. The string tugged in his hands. It danced among the clouds.
He turned the faucet handle. Water gushed into the sink. He rinsed the dirty dishes. He stacked them to dry.
The seed pod dried in the sun. It split open with a snap. Seeds scattered across the ground. New plants sprouted nearby.
She poured sand into the funnel. It streamed into the bottle. The layers stacked in colors. She sealed the bottle tight.
The moth circled the porch light. It bumped against the glass. It fluttered around in circles. It landed on the wall.
He struck two stones together. Sparks flew from the impact. The dry tinder caught fire. A small flame grew larger.
The spider climbed the water spout. Rain washed it down again. The sun dried the spout. The spider climbed once more.
She peeled the ripe banana. She sliced it into rounds. She dropped them into cereal. She poured milk over everything.
The wind gathered the fallen leaves. It swirled them into a pile. A gust scattered them again. They settled across the lawn.
He wound the fishing reel slowly. The line grew taut and tight. A large fish broke the surface. He lifted it into the boat.
The snowman stood in the yard. The afternoon sun grew warm. Its shape began to sag. It melted into a puddle.
She cracked the walnut shell. She picked out the meat inside. She added it to the bowl. She mixed it into the batter.
The candle flame caught the paper. The edges curled and blackened. Smoke rose in thin wisps. The paper crumbled to ash.
He dropped a coin in the well. It fell into the darkness. A splash echoed from below. He made a silent wish.
The clouds gathered over the hills. A cool wind swept the valley. The first raindrops began to fall. A rainbow arced across the sky.
noncausal_texts: A violin is played with a bow. A flute is played by blowing air. A drum is played by striking it. A harp is played by plucking strings.
A cow gives us milk. A hen lays eggs. A sheep provides wool. A bee makes honey.
Lions live in grasslands. Penguins live in cold regions. Camels live in deserts. Monkeys live in forests.
Water freezes into ice. Iron rusts when wet. Wood floats on water. Glass shatters when dropped.
Paris is a city in France. Tokyo is a city in Japan. Cairo is a city in Egypt. Lima is a city in Peru.
A dog barks loudly. A cat meows softly. A cow moos in the field. A duck quacks near the pond.
Apples grow on trees. Carrots grow underground. Grapes grow on vines. Pumpkins grow on the ground.
The sun gives off heat. The moon reflects sunlight. Stars twinkle in the night. Comets travel through space.
Bees collect pollen from flowers. Spiders spin webs to catch prey. Ants build large colonies. Butterflies drink nectar with their tongues.
A hammer drives in nails. A saw cuts through wood. A screwdriver turns screws. A wrench tightens bolts.
Sharks live in the ocean. Frogs live near ponds. Eagles nest on high cliffs. Rabbits dig burrows in fields.
Cheese is made from milk. Bread is made from flour. Wine is made from grapes. Chocolate is made from cocoa.
A square has four equal sides. A triangle has three straight sides. A hexagon has six flat sides. A pentagon has five joined sides.
Snakes have no legs. Spiders have eight legs. Insects have six legs. Birds have two legs.
Copper conducts electricity well. Rubber blocks electric current. Gold resists tarnishing. Aluminum is very lightweight.
Roses have thorns on their stems. Cacti store water in their trunks. Ferns grow in shady places. Sunflowers turn toward the sun.
Whales breathe through blowholes. Fish breathe through gills. Frogs breathe through their skin. Humans breathe through lungs.
Tigers have orange striped fur. Zebras have black and white stripes. Leopards have spotted coats. Elephants have thick gray skin.
A doctor treats sick patients. A teacher educates young students. A chef prepares tasty meals. A pilot flies large airplanes.
Oxygen is a gas we breathe. Gold is a shiny yellow metal. Salt is a white crystal. Sand is made of tiny grains.
The heart pumps blood around the body. The lungs take in fresh air. The stomach digests our food. The brain controls our movements.
Owls hunt during the night. Eagles soar high in the sky. Ducks swim across quiet ponds. Woodpeckers drill into tree bark.
Bananas are yellow when ripe. Limes are green and sour. Blueberries are small and round. Cherries are red and sweet.
Rain falls from the clouds. Snow covers the ground in winter. Fog hangs low in the valleys. Wind moves through the trees.
A clock shows the time. A compass points north. A thermometer measures temperature. A ruler measures length.
Kangaroos hop on strong legs. Dolphins swim in the sea. Cheetahs run very fast. Sloths move quite slowly.
Deserts receive very little rain. Rainforests stay warm and wet. Mountains rise high above the land. Oceans cover most of the earth.
A car runs on four wheels. A boat floats on the water. A plane flies through the air. A train rolls along the tracks.
Cotton comes from a plant. Wool comes from sheep. Silk comes from silkworms. Leather comes from animal hides.
Bats sleep hanging upside down. Squirrels store nuts for winter. Beavers build dams in rivers. Turtles carry shells on their backs.
Coffee has a bitter taste. Lemons have a sour flavor. Honey tastes very sweet. Chili peppers taste quite spicy.
A library holds many books. A hospital cares for the sick. A bakery sells fresh bread. A museum displays old artifacts.
The Nile flows through Egypt. The Amazon runs through Brazil. The Thames passes through London. The Danube crosses through Europe.
Diamonds are very hard. Feathers are very soft. Iron is quite heavy. Foam is very light.
Ducks have webbed feet. Cats have sharp claws. Horses have solid hooves. Parrots have curved beaks.
Summer brings warm sunshine. Winter brings cold snow. Spring brings blooming flowers. Autumn brings falling leaves.
Bears hibernate through the winter. Geese fly south in autumn. Salmon swim upstream to spawn. Crickets chirp on summer nights.
Glass is a transparent material. Wood is an opaque material. Ice has a slippery surface. Sandpaper has a rough surface.
Ostriches cannot fly at all. Hummingbirds can hover in place. Falcons dive at great speeds. Pelicans scoop fish in their beaks.
Milk is a white liquid. Coal is a black solid. Honey is a golden syrup. Vinegar is a sour liquid.
A guitar has six strings. A piano has many keys. A trumpet has three valves. A drum has a stretched skin.
Elephants are the largest land animals. Hummingbirds are among the smallest birds. Giraffes are the tallest animals. Cheetahs are the fastest runners.
Volcanoes erupt with molten lava. Earthquakes shake the ground. Tornadoes spin in violent funnels. Floods cover the land with water.
Grass is usually green. Snow is usually white. Coal is usually black. Blood is usually red.
Owls have excellent night vision. Dogs have a strong sense of smell. Cats have very flexible bodies. Bats use sound to navigate.
Wheat is ground into flour. Sugarcane is pressed for sugar. Olives are crushed for oil. Cocoa beans are made into chocolate.
Mercury is a liquid metal. Helium is a light gas. Carbon can form hard diamonds. Sodium reacts with water.
A spider has eight legs. A butterfly has four wings. A beetle has a hard shell. A grasshopper has strong hind legs.
Sharks have rows of sharp teeth. Elephants have very long trunks. Rhinos have thick pointed horns. Giraffes have very long necks.
Bread rises with yeast. Jelly sets with gelatin. Cake becomes fluffy with baking powder. Cheese thickens with rennet.
Tokyo is the capital of Japan. Ottawa is the capital of Canada. Nairobi is the capital of Kenya. Brasilia is the capital of Brazil.
A telescope views distant stars. A microscope reveals tiny cells. A stethoscope hears the heartbeat. A periscope sees above the water.
Penguins waddle on the land. Kangaroos hop across the plains. Snakes slither on the ground. Eagles glide through the air.
Maple trees produce sweet sap. Oak trees grow small acorns. Pine trees stay green all year. Willow trees have drooping branches.
Rubber bounces when it is dropped. Clay molds into many shapes. Steel bends under great force. Chalk crumbles into fine powder.
A river flows to the sea. A lake holds still water. A waterfall drops over cliffs. A spring bubbles from the ground.
Cats groom themselves often. Dogs wag their tails. Birds preen their feathers. Rabbits twitch their noses.
Salt dissolves in water. Oil floats on water. Sugar sweetens our drinks. Sand sinks to the bottom.
Bees live in busy hives. Ants live in large colonies. Wasps build paper nests. Termites build tall mounds.
Gold is a precious metal. Marble is a smooth stone. Bamboo is a fast-growing plant. Amber is fossilized tree resin.
The Sahara is a vast desert. The Pacific is a huge ocean. The Alps are a mountain range. The Congo is a dense rainforest.
Camels store fat in their humps. Polar bears have thick warm fur. Frogs have moist smooth skin. Chameleons can change their color.
A pencil writes in graphite. A pen writes in ink. A crayon writes in wax. A marker writes in dye.
Lemons contain vitamin C. Carrots contain vitamin A. Fish contain healthy oils. Spinach contains plenty of iron.
Peacocks display colorful tails. Cardinals have bright red feathers. Flamingos stand on one leg. Toucans have large orange beaks.
Wind turbines make electricity. Solar panels absorb sunlight. Dams generate hydropower. Windmills grind grain into flour.
A knife slices the bread. A fork holds the food. A spoon scoops the soup. A whisk beats the eggs.
Octopuses have eight arms. Starfish have five arms. Jellyfish have long tentacles. Crabs have hard claws.
Honey never spoils. Vinegar tastes very sharp. Butter melts when heated. Flour is a fine powder.
Mount Everest stands very tall. The Dead Sea sits very low. The Nile stretches very long. The Pacific runs very deep.
Snails carry shells on their backs. Worms have no bones. Crabs walk sideways. Frogs leap with strong legs.
Iron is attracted to magnets. Copper turns green over time. Silver reflects light brightly. Lead is a very dense metal.
A cactus stores water inside. A mushroom grows without sunlight. A vine climbs up walls. A moss covers damp rocks.
Bees dance to share directions. Wolves howl to their pack. Elephants trumpet with their trunks. Dolphins click to communicate.
Sunlight helps the plants grow. Rain waters the dry fields. Wind spreads the tiny seeds. Soil holds the plant roots.
Apples can be red or green. Oranges are round and sweet. Lemons are yellow and sour. Peaches have soft fuzzy skin.
A violin belongs to the string family. A trumpet belongs to the brass family. A flute belongs to the woodwind family. A cymbal belongs to the percussion family.
Bats are nocturnal creatures. Bees are busy pollinators. Sharks are ocean predators. Turtles are slow reptiles.
Granite forms from cooled magma. Limestone forms from seashells. Sandstone forms from pressed sand. Marble forms from heated rock.
Cats have sharp retractable claws. Horses have long flowing manes. Pigs have short curly tails. Goats have small pointed horns.
Sugar comes in white crystals. Pepper comes as small grains. Cinnamon comes as brown bark. Ginger comes as a knobby root.
Frogs lay eggs in water. Birds lay eggs in nests. Turtles lay eggs in sand. Spiders lay eggs in silk sacs.
A castle has tall stone walls. A lighthouse guides passing ships. A bridge crosses over rivers. A tunnel passes through mountains.
Sunflowers have bright yellow petals. Lavender has a soothing scent. Tulips bloom in early spring. Daisies have white round petals.
Ice is frozen water. Steam is heated water. Rain is falling water. Dew is morning water.
Owls rotate their heads widely. Giraffes have very long tongues. Snakes smell with their tongues. Frogs catch flies with their tongues.
Wood burns to make fire. Coal powers old steam engines. Oil fuels many vehicles. Gas heats many homes.
A wagon has four wheels. A bicycle has two wheels. A unicycle has one wheel. A tricycle has three wheels.
Elephants drink with their trunks. Anteaters eat with long tongues. Woodpeckers peck with strong beaks. Beavers chew with sharp teeth.
Salt makes food taste savory. Sugar makes food taste sweet. Lemon makes food taste tangy. Pepper makes food taste hot.
The lion is a big cat. The wolf is a wild dog. The eagle is a large bird. The shark is a big fish.
A stove heats up the food. A fridge keeps the food cold. A blender mixes the ingredients. An oven bakes the bread.
Redwoods grow extremely tall. Bonsai trees stay very small. Baobabs have wide thick trunks. Palms have long leafy fronds.
Cotton feels soft to touch. Sandpaper feels rough to touch. Silk feels smooth to touch. Wool feels warm to touch.
Bees make honey in hives. Silkworms spin fine silk threads. Spiders weave sticky webs. Ants dig underground tunnels.
Australia is a large island continent. Antarctica is covered in thick ice. Africa has vast open savannas. Europe has many small countries.
A candle gives off light. A fan moves the air around. A mirror reflects an image. A magnet attracts nearby metal.
Chickens cluck in the coop. Cows graze in the pasture. Pigs roll in the mud. Sheep gather in tight flocks.
Sound travels through the air. Light travels very quickly. Heat rises steadily upward. Gravity pulls objects down.
Rubies are deep red gems. Emeralds are green precious stones. Sapphires are blue and hard. Pearls form inside oysters.
